content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 15, 1993

Judith Sproul Davis, Esq.
Nielsen, Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller & Naylor
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Your Request For
Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-119

Dear Ms. Davis:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because your letter does not
identify the person with duties under the Act on whose behalf you
are writing, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.2/

Your letter of March 26, 1993, correctly summarizes my advice
that a payment made by a hotel in settlement of a law suit filed
against it by a candidate's controlled committee is not a
contribution under the Act so long as the settlement is not made
for political purposes and is of the type that would be made by
the hotel in resolving any law suit brought against the hotel by
any hotel customer.

If you have additional questions concerning this matter,
please give me a call at 916/322-5662.

Sincerely,

Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations. Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: Your letter of March 26, 1993, correctly summarizes my advice that a payment made by a hotel in settlement of a law suit filed against it by a candidate's controlled committee is not a contribution under the Act so long as the settlement is not made for political purposes and is of the type that would be made by the hotel in resolving any law suit brought against the hotel by any hotel customer.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a payment made by a hotel in settlement of a lawsuit filed against it by a candidate's controlled committee constitutes a contribution under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A settlement payment made by a hotel to resolve a lawsuit filed by a candidate's controlled committee is not a contribution under the Political Reform Act if the payment is not made for political purposes and is of the type the hotel would make in resolving any similar lawsuit.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal question/conclusion sections present; document is confirmation of prior telephone advice. Informal assistance letter, not formal written advice (no immunity provided per footnote 2).

QUESTION: Whether a payment made by a hotel in settlement of a lawsuit filed against it by a candidate's controlled committee constitutes a contribution under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Your letter of March 26, 1993, correctly summarizes my advice that a payment made by a hotel in settlement of a law suit filed against it by a candidate's controlled committee is not a contribution under the Act so long as the settlement is not made for political purposes and is of the type that would be made by the hotel in resolving any law suit brought against the hotel by any hotel customer.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms informal telephone advice that a hotel's settlement payment to a candidate's controlled committee in a lawsuit is not a contribution under the Political Reform Act, provided the settlement is not made for political purposes and is consistent with how the hotel would settle similar lawsuits.
